title: quercus × vaga
intro: Quercus × vaga is a hybrid oak tree, resulting from the cross between two species of oak trees, Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and skin problems. The leaves and bark of Quercus × vaga have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Quercus × vaga, including teas and infusions, have been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations.
